pr.title: Attempt to resolve the mystery of HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty
pr.author: lukechampine

summary: Renamed ftCo_800C6AFC to the real HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty symbol at 0x800C6AFC and centralized many matching-only JObj matrix-dirty setter variants in jobj.h, replacing per-TU fake helpers across fighter, game-mode, item, stage, menu, and VI code. The PR intentionally kept some weird parenthesized calls and WithMtxDirty variants because full unification with the normal HSD_JObjSet* helpers was not proven to match.
key_files: config/GALE01/symbols.txt - renamed ftCo_800C6AFC to HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty at .text:0x800C6AFC
config/GALE01/scratches.txt - updated scratch matches for the same 0x800C6AFC symbol rename
src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.h - added the parenthesized real HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty declaration plus WithMtxDirty and OutOfLine helper variants
src/melee/ft/ft_0C31.c - renamed the ftCo_800C6AFC function definition to void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj*) and replaced local fake translate helpers
src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs.c - deleted local ftKb fake JObj helpers and used baselib/jobj.h helpers directly
src/melee/gm/gmregtyfall.static.h - deleted an 87-line fake helper header
src/melee/gm/gm_1A4C.c and src/melee/gm/gmregtyfall.c - switched fake_tyfall setters to centralized HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty helpers
src/melee/it/items/itlinkarrow.c, itlinkbomb.c, itbombhei.c, itkyasarin.c - removed or bypassed local fake HSD_JObj helper duplicates
what_changed: The global symbol and scratch records now identify address 0x800C6AFC as HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty instead of ftCo_800C6AFC.
ft_0C31.c now defines void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(HSD_JObj* jobj) where ftCo_800C6AFC used to live, and ft_0C31.h no longer exposes ftCo_800C6AFC.

jobj.h gained a real-function declaration using a parenthesized name, HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty out-of-line wrappers, and matching-only WithMtxDirty variants for rotation, scale, translate, add-rotation-X, and add-translation-Y setters.
Several files stopped including or defining local fake JObj setters that manually wrote transform fields and called ftCo_800C6AFC.

ftKb_SpecialNNs.c removed fake ftKb_JObjGetScale, ftKb_JObjSetScale, ftKb_JObjMtxIsDirty, and ftKb_JObjSetRotationY helpers and replaced uses with HSD_JObjGetScale, HSD_JObjSetScale, and HSD_JObjSetRotationY.
gmregtyfall.static.h was deleted, and its fake_tyfall_HSD_JObjSetTranslate/Rotation/Scale helpers were replaced by HSD_JObjSet*WithMtxDirty calls in gmregtyfall.c and gm_1A4C.c.
Some remaining local inline JObj helpers were not fully unified; their dirty call target was changed from ftCo_800C6AFC to (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj), preserving their local matching shape.
smart_moves: Identified that the fighter-named ftCo_800C6AFC was actually the JObj matrix-dirty function emitted in the ft range, then renamed the symbol instead of continuing to route baselib JObj behavior through an ft/ name.
Moved repeated fake helper logic into jobj.h as named WithMtxDirty variants, reducing per-TU copies while preserving the assert line numbers and JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT checks used for matching.
Used (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) to call the real function symbol without expanding the existing function-like macro HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(jobj).
Added HSD_JObjSetTranslateWithMtxDirtyOutOfLine and a leaf wrapper for cases where the target needed an out-of-line branch rather than the ordinary inline/macro expansion.
Deleted an entire static header, gmregtyfall.static.h, once its only purpose was replaced by centralized JObj helpers.
Only used the WithMtxDirty workaround where needed; ftKirby call sites that matched with normal HSD_JObjGetScale, HSD_JObjSetScale, and HSD_JObjSetRotationY were simplified to the real helpers.
decomp_lessons: A function that appears in an ft address range is not necessarily fighter-owned; header emission, inlining decisions, weak/out-of-line behavior, and linker order can place a baselib helper in the first TU that needs it.

Function-like macros can be deliberately bypassed by parenthesizing the macro name before the call, which matters here because HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(jobj) and (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) produce different behavior.

When decomp code accumulates multiple fake helpers with the same transform-write-and-dirty pattern, centralizing them in the owning header makes future matching work easier to search and less error-prone.

Keep normal semantic helpers and matching-only helpers separate: the ordinary HSD_JObjSet* versions dirty through the current macro/inline path, while the WithMtxDirty variants intentionally call the real out-of-line symbol.

Preserving HSD_ASSERT file names, line numbers, and condition strings is part of matching; the new variants kept jobj.h assertion lines such as rotation quaternion checks and translate/scale null checks.
The PR did not prove every fake helper could be removed, so future work should treat WithMtxDirty as an intermediate matching tactic rather than final API design.
naming_conventions: Use HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty for the 0x800C6AFC symbol, not ftCo_800C6AFC.
Use the suffix WithMtxDirty for JObj setter variants that should write the transform field and call the real matrix-dirty function symbol.
Use OutOfLine and OutOfLineLeaf suffixes when an extra wrapper is needed to force an out-of-line call shape.
Prefer HSD_JObj* helper names over per-file fake_HSD_JObj*, fake_tyfall_HSD_JObj*, or ftKb_JObj* duplicates when the behavior is shared baselib JObj behavior.
A parenthesized declaration or call such as void (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(...) and (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) is a meaningful matching idiom here, not style noise.
Avoid retaining fighter-prefixed names for baselib functions simply because the emitted body lands in an ft translation unit.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Use (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) to avoid expansion of the fake HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty(jobj) macro and generate a call to the real function.
Use WithMtxDirty setters when the regular HSD_JObjSet* inline/macro path dirties through HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtySub and does not match target code that branches to 0x800C6AFC.
Use HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLineLeaf plus HSD_JObjSetMtxDirtyOutOfLine when a call site needs an extra non-inlined layer to match.
Preserve the JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT guard before dirtying and the JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION assert for rotation component setters.
If full helper unification changes codegen, a narrower replacement of ftCo_800C6AFC(jobj) with (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty)(jobj) inside existing local inline helpers may be the safer incremental match.
Do not assume macro wrapping is cosmetic in this codebase; reviewer and author discussion confirmed parenthesized macro calls affected behavior.
review_feedback: ribbanya asked why the called function was wrapped in parentheses.
ribbanya suggested the likely explanation: if HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty is in jobj.h, linker order and the first non-inlined call in ft_0C31.c could cause the compiler to emit the function body in that TU.
lukechampine reported that wrapping a macro in parentheses when calling it gave different behavior from calling it unwrapped.
ribbanya stated that the HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty macro is for sure fake, but approved merging the cleaned-up duplicates for now despite the weird parentheses.

decomp.dev reported a match regression on the inspected commit, including ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim dropping below 100% and it_802EDDC0 regressing in an unmatched item; the PR was still merged as cleanup.
follow_up_queries: Search for any remaining ftCo_800C6AFC references and verify they should all become HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty or parenthesized HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty calls.
Audit remaining local HSD_JObjSet*_Fake helpers that now call (HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty) and see whether they can be replaced by centralized WithMtxDirty variants.
Investigate whether the fake HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty macro can eventually be removed or converted without match regressions.
Confirm the linker-order or weak-emission explanation for why HSD_JObjSetMtxDirty is emitted at 0x800C6AFC inside the ft_0C31 range.
Revisit the decomp.dev-reported ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim and it_802EDDC0 regressions and determine whether they remained after merge.
Search for WithMtxDirty usages once adjacent JObj/lbcoll code improves, and try converting them back to ordinary HSD_JObjSet* helpers where matching permits.
